text:
There was a peculiar flower that bloomed only once every hundred years. It wasn't your ordinary flower. It had eyes that sparkled with intelligence. People called it the "Eyesweeper."

Everyone knew the secret. Whenever someone looked into the Eyesweeper, their dreams would come alive. They couldn't escape it—it was a sort of dream incubator. They dreamt of future inventions, lost loves, and forgotten adventures.

One sleepy evening, a young boy named Oliver awoke with a brilliant idea. He wanted to create a tool that would clean windows. He would call it the "Dreamweaver."

Excitedly, he dashed downstairs to talk to his favorite antique bookshop owner, Mrs. Hawthorne. "Mrs. Hawthorne," Oliver gasped, "I've got the most fantastic invention!"

Mrs. Hawthorne leaned closer. "And what might it be?"

"A window cleaner... that cleans in dreams." Oliver beamed.

Mrs. Hawthorne nodded thoughtfully
